kernel: N149 PHILB -- N107 PY 4-warp 64x64 HGEMM + Hilbert-curve d2xy CTA-swizzle (Pattern B)
host_driver: host_hilbert.c == N157 host_one.c with Hilbert p x p launch grid (p=next_pow2(M/64)); kernel internally d2xy + padding early-return
hypothesis: Hilbert's tighter Manhattan-adjacent 2D blob pushes L2 hit above super-block's 87% plateau, explaining N149's +26-35% perf delta over super-block at the cliff.
verdict: HYPOTHESIS CONFIRMED -- Hilbert L2 hit 96.5-96.8% EXCEEDS super-block 87% plateau by ~+9.4-9.9 pts, approaching M=4096 near-perfect 98%. Locality ladder 56 -> 87 -> 97% is monotone in every memory metric.
prediction: Hilbert L2 hit > super-block 87%; if ~equal, gain would be from coalescing/DRAM-pattern instead.
ncu_replay_caveat: ncu kernel-replay wall (0.7-4.6 TFLOPS) is NOT real perf; counters are replay-invariant. Real perf = N149 result.json + this cycle nsys 20-rep.

nsys_sanity_tflops.note: matches N149 real perf (58.49/59.48) within <2% -- no replay contamination

causal_chain: Hilbert d2xy CTA order -> concurrent CTAs touch a Manhattan-adjacent 2D blob (tighter than super-block's 256x256 row strip) -> L2 hit 87 -> 97% -> DRAM bytes 3.3-3.6x less than super-block (and 11.8-12.9x less than no-swizzle) -> DRAM un-saturates to ~9% / 59-63 GB/s (nearly idle) -> warp-cycles/inst 43->40, eligible warps 0.33->0.35-0.36 -> compute SM 38->43% -> +26-35% real throughput over super-block.
controlled_ab: kernel body byte-identical across no-swizzle/super-block/Hilbert (inst_executed within 0.5%, occupancy identical ~66%); ONLY changed variable = CTA visitation order (+ Hilbert p x p launch grid with cheap padding early-return). Isolates L2 reuse as the sole cause of the locality ladder.
honest_caveats: Hilbert L2 hit 96.5-96.8% > super-block 87% CONFIRMS the tighter-2D-blob hypothesis. Did NOT reach M=4096's 98% -- full A+B working set (144-256MB) still >> 32MB L2; Hilbert bounds the concurrent blob, not the whole matrix. Explains residual ratio gap to cuBLAS (0.834/0.847).

DRAM-bytes delta (3.3-3.6x less than super-block) is even larger than the L2-hit delta alone predicts -- higher L2 hit at the cliff has super-linear leverage on DRAM bytes because each avoided miss removes a full sector fetch from a >4.5x-L2 working set. Gain is BOTH predicted-locality AND sharper DRAM-traffic drop, one causal chain.
Occupancy UNCHANGED ~66% (register-limited 64 regs/thd) across all 3 variants -- recovery is a memory-latency/scheduler story, NOT occupancy.
ncu run on ubu-2 (same as N157); N140 no-swizzle baseline on ubu-1 (same GPU model+driver). N157 validated cross-host via M=4096 control (L2 within 0.03 pts).
Hilbert padding CTAs early-return cheaply: inst_executed within 0.5% of super-block -> p x p launch does not inflate work.
